rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenylation/cyclization of N-allylthioamides with diselenides to form selenyl-substituted 4,5-dihydrothiazoles
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of diaryl diselenide cleaves Se-Se bond to give a selenyl radical and a selenide anion; the selenide anion is reoxidized at the anode to a selenyl radical, which adds to the alkene; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation.

key_intermediates.name: Alkyl C-radical from selenyl addition to allyl thioamide (C)

electron_flow: Diselenide is reduced at the cathode to a selenyl radical A and selenide anion B; B is reoxidized at the anode back to A; A adds to the allyl C=C of the thioamide generating a β-selenyl C-radical C, which is anodically oxidized to carbocation D; intramolecular 5-exo-trig attack of the thiocarbonyl S followed by deprotonation furnishes the dihydrothiazole, with cathodic H+ reduction (½ H2) closing the redox balance.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a formed in the presence of 2 equiv TEMPO, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclopropyl-substituted allyl thioamide (1x, radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: 3x obtained in 54% yield with the cyclopropyl group intact, excluding an intramolecular thiyl radical cyclization/selenylation mechanism

probe_reagents.name: Diphenyl diselenide (cyclic voltammetry)
probe_reagents.observation: Reduction peak observed at -0.51 V, indicating facile cathodic reduction of 2a

notes: An alternative pathway involving intramolecular thiyl radical cyclization followed by selenylation was explicitly excluded by the cyclopropyl radical-clock experiment (ring-opening not observed).
